auth_notes: Most providers (Change Healthcare, Availity, Eligible) require an OAuth2 flow to obtain a bearer token using a Client ID and Secret, or a static X-API-KEY header for simpler REST implementations.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; typically 10-50 requests per second for standard tiers.
gotchas: Payloads often require specific X12 EDI 270/271 mapping logic even when using JSON wrappers.
HIPAA compliance requires n8n to be self-hosted or on a HIPAA-compliant cloud tier to handle PHI.
Payer IDs are inconsistent across different clearinghouses and require a lookup step.
research_notes: The implementation will require an HTTP Request node to authenticate and retrieve a token, followed by a second HTTP Request node to POST the eligibility inquiry. Users must handle the complex nested JSON response to extract specific benefit flags (e.g., 'active' status, co-pay amounts).
